description:
Automated translation (refer to source for accuracy)

2026 Cultural Heritage Walk: "Enjoying Hanno's Geography and Geology"

【Date & Time】Saturday, May 23, 2026 (Reiwa 8), 9:15 a.m.–12:30 p.m. (Registration starts at 9:00 a.m.; event proceeds in light rain, cancelled in severe weather)

【Meeting Point】Moto-Kaji Station, Seibu Ikebukuro Line (assembly and dispersal)

【What You'll Do】Walk the Kaji Hills Course (about 3 kilometers) while observing fossil footprints of Deinotherium (Akebono Elephant) in the Iruma River riverbed, and rock formations of the Hanno and Butsuji layers that make up the Kaji Hills. Expert commentary provided by the Kanto Plain Western Rim Hills Research Group.

【Who Can Join】High school students and older

【Capacity】20 people (advance reservation required; first-come, first-served)

【Fee】500 yen (includes insurance)

【How to Apply】From Friday, May 1, 2026, register by phone, email, or in person at Hanno Central District Administrative Center

【Important Notes】Please wear comfortable, moveable clothing including a hat and easy-walking shoes. Bring drinking water and other heat-illness prevention supplies on your own.

Capacity has been reached; registration closed as of May 20, 2026.

Source (Japanese): https://www.city.hanno.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/kyoikubu/hakubutsukan/bunkazaitanto/bunkazai/13272.html
summary: ℹ️ Cultural Heritage Walk 2026 scheduled for May 23 (Registration closed as of May 20)
